cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Salesforce's slow Agentforce adoption exposes enterprise agentic AI readiness gap
stories.plain: Salesforce's own struggles to get customers onto Agentforce reveal a wider truth: agentic AI stalls when underlying data quality is poor. The piece shows that buying the platform is the easy part — fixing the messy customer data and processes that agents depend on is the real blocker most enterprises haven't solved yet.

stories.head: 156 AI & Technology Statistics Marketers Should Know in 2026
stories.plain: A fully sourced compilation of 156 statistics covering AI adoption rates, agentic AI growth, ROI benchmarks, and governance trends for 2026. Useful as a reference document when making the case internally for AI investment or when assessing how your organisation compares to industry norms.

stories.head: Tec-Do launches Navos 2.0 multi-agent marketing system at WAIC
stories.plain: Navos 2.0 is a multi-agent system that plans and executes marketing tasks across office productivity tools — a sign that agentic marketing software is moving beyond chatbots into systems that chain decisions and actions end-to-end. Its debut at WAIC signals growing non-US competition in the marketing AI space.

stories.head: Salesforce's Agentforce struggles expose marketing's agentic AI readiness gap
stories.plain: Salesforce's slower-than-expected Agentforce adoption is a signal, not just a company story: enterprises lack the clean data and operational foundations that agentic AI requires. This matters for anyone buying or building AI marketing systems because it reframes the blocker from technology to data quality and org readiness.

stories.head: Why AI-ready data infrastructure is so hard to build — and how to start
stories.plain: Most companies stall because they try to fix all their data before touching AI. This piece argues for starting with one high-value problem to prove the model and build momentum. Practical framing for marketers deciding where to invest in data pipelines first, directly affecting how AI projects get scoped and funded.

stories.head: 91% of marketers claim an AI search strategy but most are flying blind, survey finds
stories.plain: A 600-person survey reveals a stark gap: nearly all marketers say they have a generative engine optimization strategy, yet 62% admit they are already behind competitors. The data shows confidence is decoupled from execution, making this a useful benchmark for teams assessing their own AI visibility investments.

stories.head: 5 questions to ask AI vendors before you buy anything
stories.plain: A buyer's checklist covering business-value proof, data ownership and privacy policies, real customer evidence, and implementation costs. Directly useful for anyone evaluating MarTech-AI purchases — helps cut through vendor hype with concrete due-diligence questions before contracts are signed.

stories.head: Agentic AI hype vs. reality: why enterprise-wide ROI remains elusive
stories.plain: Despite excitement since early 2024, no publicly known example of enterprise-wide agentic AI deployment exists — every implementation stays within a single function or business unit. The piece diagnoses why and offers a framework for generating tangible ROI, relevant to anyone governing or sponsoring AI rollouts.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI's Reality Gap
body: The promise of autonomous AI agents doing marketing work is colliding with messy organizational reality. Salesforce's public stumbles are the clearest signal yet that deploying agentic AI at enterprise scale surfaces problems — data silos, unclear accountability, brittle integrations — that no vendor roadmap anticipated.

The decision that changes: buying an agentic AI platform is no longer primarily a technology choice; it is a process-redesign commitment. Teams that skip the process work will spend the next year debugging autonomous failures rather than harvesting autonomous wins.
ev: Agile Brand · Beyond agentic hype & ROI

themes.head: Content Parity Kills the Moat
body: When every brand can produce polished copy, images, and video with a single prompt or one uploaded photo, content quality stops being a differentiator. The Scribewise research finding that 91% of marketers claim an AI search strategy — yet most are barely started — reinforces that volume and format are now table stakes, not edges.

The implication for budget holders is stark: shifting spend toward distribution, audience data, and human creative direction will matter more than incremental content production tools. The question is no longer 'can we make enough content?' but 'what makes ours worth choosing?'
ev: MarTech · Content no longer competitive advantage
MarTech Series · 91% claim AI search strategy
MarTech Series · PixPix one-image e-commerce workflow

themes.head: The Data Infrastructure Bottleneck
body: AI adoption has hit 90% at the tool level, but the underlying data infrastructure most stacks run on was never designed for machine learning pipelines. Fragmented 'Frankenstacks' — layers of point solutions stitched together over years — are the structural reason AI investments underdeliver, and vendors like Creatio are now explicitly targeting that complexity as their market.

For planners: the ROI ceiling on any AI initiative is set by data readiness, not model capability. Auditing data quality, labeling pipelines, and integration architecture before the next AI purchase is no longer optional hygiene — it is the primary lever.

themes.head: Measurement & the Spend-Performance Confusion
body: Two separate pieces this month converged on the same uncomfortable finding: marketers know which activities improve paid media ROI but systematically underinvest in them, and they routinely conflate rising ad spend with rising marketing performance. AI has accelerated the problem by making it easier to pour budget into automated bidding without interrogating whether the underlying signal is sound.

The corrective is a measurement-first posture — defining what 'performance' means before allocating budget, not after. That means investing in incrementality testing, media mix modeling, and creative analytics even when they slow down the deployment of shiny new AI features.
ev: MarTech · Don't confuse ad spend with performance
MarTech · Underinvestment in paid media ROI drivers

themes.head: The Human-Skill Deficit Accelerating Under AI
body: If AI handles execution — drafting, segmenting, optimizing — junior marketers never develop the judgment those tasks used to build. The field is beginning to reckon with a skills pipeline problem: who trains the next generation of strategists when the apprenticeship work has been automated away? The generational divide in AI adoption compounds this, as adoption patterns differ sharply by age cohort and experience level.

This is a strategic workforce risk, not just an HR footnote. Brands that treat AI purely as a headcount reduction tool are quietly eroding the institutional knowledge needed to supervise, correct, and improve the AI itself — creating a dependency that grows more fragile over time.
ev: Agile Brand · Generational divide in AI adoption
Agile Brand · Human edge in automated CX

month.takeaway: Across every signal this month, the pattern is the same: AI is exposing the weaknesses that were always there — in data infrastructure, measurement discipline, content strategy, and human skill development — and the marketers who thrive will be those who use the AI moment to fix the foundations, not paper over them.
